problems.title: products of the 2-full part over short intervals
problems.status: OPEN — asymptotic (‘cannot be resolved with a finite computation’); not a Leibniz solve target
problems.apa: Erdős, P., & Graham, R. L. (1980). Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory (Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathématique No. 28). Université de Genève.
problems.non_vacuity: ∀k∀ε∃C∀n form of ≪ n^{2+o(1)}; the o(1) is essential (van Doorn: fails ≪_k n² for k≥3), so the statement is not trivially true and the quantifier order is load-bearing.

problems.title: the thirteenth powers have a tiling complement in ℤ
problems.status: RESOLVED affirmatively (Peng, Tao, Wang, Yu & Liu, 2026)
problems.apa: Peng, B., Tao, R., Wang, S., Yu, H., & Liu, D. (2026). Erdős problem 477 [Preprint]. In pipeline-math. GitHub. https://github.com/Pengbinghui/pipeline-math
problems.anchor: tiling_sanity proved (ℤ = univ ⊕ {0}) — the definition captures *unique* representation
problems.non_vacuity: ∃A, ∀n ∃! (a,b) …; the uniqueness (∃!) is the whole content — a plain ∃ would be trivially true, so the ∃! is load-bearing.

reading: Erdős statement-formalization lane: faithful Lean statements of Erdős problems (NOT solutions — the DB is mostly asymptotic and kernel-undecidable), each passing the faithfulness gate (elaborates + a faithfulness anchor + a non-vacuity note). 367 is an OPEN asymptotic bound (statement only); 477 is a resolved combinatorial problem (bridge to the counterexample domain). Not submitting to erdosproblems.com (their AI policy); the url is attribution. GREEN = every registered statement passes the gate.
